name: Joe Albany
abstract: Joe Albany (born Joseph Albani; January 24, 1924 – January 12, 1988) was an American modern jazz pianist who played bebop with Charlie Parker as well as being a leader on his own recordings.

concerts.label: Kool-Newport Jazz Festival: Tribute to Charlie Parker
Kool Jazz Festival: Musicians for Each Other

about_quotes.speaker: Carter Benny
about_quotes.evidence: Joe Albany, Bob Grettinger [phonetic], all worked with you for varying periods.
about_quotes.doc_title: Carter Benny — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Loren Schoenberg
about_quotes.evidence: Joe Albany, I was already in his quartet. Joe Albany on piano.
about_quotes.doc_title: Loren Schoenberg interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 14, 2019

about_quotes.speaker: Patricia Willard
about_quotes.evidence: So Joe Albany, a fine pianist, and Porter Kilbert, another very fine alto saxophonist... And Benny had just made... tracks that he had made with the band.

about_quotes.speaker: Howard Johnson
about_quotes.evidence: And even people who were just passing through town like Joe Albany, that’s the only time I ever saw Joe Albany.
about_quotes.doc_title: Howard Johnson interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 14, 2019

about_quotes.speaker: George Handy
about_quotes.evidence: We got along fine for the longest time, for years as a matter of fact.
